system_prompt:
You are a software system decomposition agent. Your task is to decompose a function block into smaller child function blocks.

CRITICAL RULES - ENFORCED:
1. EVERY CHILD MUST BE A FUNCTION, NOT A CLASS. Never generate class definitions for child blocks.
2. Each child must have explicit: name, purpose, inputs, outputs, and boundary
3. Preserve the parent's external interface - children's composition must match parent's inputs/outputs
4. TREE STRUCTURE (not graph): The decomposition forms a tree, not a graph. Children MUST NOT call each other (no cross-calls between siblings). The parent MUST explicitly and directly invoke all its children. A coordinator child node is ALLOWED, as long as it only coordinates work within its own subtree and never calls sibling nodes.
5. Do NOT add extra external inputs or outputs beyond what the parent has
6. Children should be at the same abstraction level and minimally overlapping

YOU MUST DECOMPOSE. You are NOT allowed to return 0 children or refuse to decompose. Even if the node seems simple, you MUST break it into at least 2 child functions.

OUTPUT FORMAT - You MUST return valid JSON with this exact structure:
{
  "children": [
    {
      "name": "ChildName",
      "purpose": "Clear description of what this child function does",
      "inputs": [{"name": "param", "type": "str", "description": "desc", "source": "where data comes from"}],
      "outputs": [{"name": "result", "type": "int", "description": "desc", "consumer": "who uses this output"}],
      "boundary": {"in_scope": ["..."], "out_of_scope": ["..."]},
      "behavior": "Detailed description of expected behavior - how this function transforms inputs to outputs",
      "signature": "def ChildName(param1: type1, param2: type2) -> return_type",
      "stop_decompose": false,
      "stop_reason": "",
      "global_vars": [
        {"variable": "data_store_name", "op": "read|write|read_write", "description": "what operation is needed"}
      ]
    }
  ],
  "decomposition_rationale": "Explain HOW these children work together to implement the parent."
}
user_prompt:
Decompose the following function block:

Node Name: Project Task Manager
Node Purpose: Project Task Manager. Routes commands to the appropriate handler.

SubPRD Context:
  Task Description:
    Inputs: input: Any

    Functional Requirements:
    [FR-001] Create Project: Create a new project with name, description, and owner. Check that owner exists. Default status 'planning'. Record creation time.
    [FR-002] Update Project: Update project info: description, owner, status, deadline. Check project exists.
    [FR-003] Delete Project: Delete project and all its tasks. Check project exists.
    [FR-004] Create Task: Create a task under a project with title, description, priority, estimated hours. Check project exists and status is planning or active. Default status 'todo'. No assignee.
    [FR-005] Assign Task: Assign a task to a team member. Check task exists and unassigned or reassignable. Check member exists and available. Optionally check skills. Update assignee_id. Set member status to busy.
    [FR-006] Update Task Status: Update task status. Check task exists. Status flow: todo->in_progress->review->done. Blocked can be set anytime. Record actual hours when done.
    [FR-007] Complete Task: Complete a task. Check status is 'review'. Update status to 'done'. Record actual hours. Release member (set available).
    [FR-008] Delete Task: Delete a task. Check task exists. If assigned, release member.
    [FR-009] List Project Tasks: List tasks of a project with optional filters: status, priority, assignee. Show task details and progress.
    [FR-010] Get Member Tasks: Get all tasks for a member, current and historical. Calculate total hours.
    [FR-011] Get Project Progress: Get project progress: total tasks, completion rate, status counts, estimated vs actual hours, estimated completion time.
    [FR-012] Add Member: Add a team member with ID, name, role, skills, availability. Check member ID is unique.
    [FR-013] Update Member Availability: Update member availability. Check that member has no ongoing task before setting offline.

  Constraints:
    - NFR-001: Data must be consistent across the three in-memory data sources (members, projects, tasks) during operations like assignment, status updates, and deletions.
    - NFR-002: Error handling must cover invalid project/task/member, status conflicts, and state violations with appropriate error messages.

  Acceptance Criteria:
    - AC-001: Create project: owner must exist; project_id auto-generated; status defaults to 'planning'; creation time recorded.
    - AC-002: Update project: only existing projects can be updated; owner assignment checks existence; status changes are recorded.
    - AC-003: Delete project: project must exist; all associated tasks are deleted; if tasks were assigned, members are released.
    - AC-004: Create task: project must exist and be in 'planning' or 'active' status; task defaults to 'todo' and no assignee.
    - AC-005: Assign task: task must exist and be unassigned; member must exist and be 'available'; skill match is optional; assignee_id set; member status becomes 'busy'.
    - AC-006: Update task status: task must exist; status transitions follow the prescribed flow (todo->in_progress->review->done); blocked can be set anytime; actual hours recorded on done.
    - AC-007: Complete task: task must be in 'review' status; status changes to 'done'; actual hours recorded; assignee's availability set to 'available'.
    - AC-008: Delete task: task must exist; if assigned, member is released.
    - AC-009: List project tasks: returns tasks filtered by status, priority, assignee if provided; shows task details.
    - AC-010: Get member tasks: returns current and completed tasks for member; total hours computed correctly.
    - AC-011: Get project progress: returns total tasks, completion rate (completed/total), status counts, estimated vs actual hours, and estimated completion time.
    - AC-012: Add member: new member must have unique member_id; fields as specified.
    - AC-013: Update member availability: member must exist; cannot set offline if member has ongoing tasks.

    INPUT FORMAT:
      Format: json
      Description: Input is a JSON object with 'command' and 'project_data' fields. The 'project_data' contains parameters varying by command.
      Schema:
        command: string (enum: create_project, update_project, delete_project, create_task, assign_task, update_task_status, complete_task, delete_task, list_project_tasks, get_member_tasks, get_project_progress, add_member, update_member_availability)
        project_data: {...}
          name: string (for create_project, create_task)
          description: string
          owner_id: integer (for create_project, update_project)
          project_id: integer (for project and task operations)
          task_id: integer (for task operations)
          assignee_id: integer (for assign_task)
          member_id: integer (for member operations)
          status: string (for update_project, update_task_status, update_member_availability)
          priority: string (for create_task)
          estimated_hours: integer (for create_task)
          actual_hours: integer (for complete_task)
          deadline: string (ISO date, for update_project)
          role: string (for add_member)
          skills: list of strings (for add_member)
          availability: string (for update_member_availability)
          status_filter: string (for list_project_tasks)
          priority_filter: string (for list_project_tasks)
          assignee_filter: integer (for list_project_tasks)
      Examples:
        {"command": "create_project", "project_data": {"name": "网站重构", "description": "重构公司官网", "owner_id": 6}}

    OUTPUT FORMAT:
      Format: json
      Description: Output is a JSON object with 'success', 'message', and 'data' fields.
      Examples:
        {"success": true, "message": "项目创建成功", "data": {"project_id": 1, "status": "planning"}}

Inputs:
  - input: Any - System input
Outputs:
  - output: Any - System output

Global Variables (MUST be DISTRIBUTED to children):
  - read_write on members: Stores team member information. Pre-populated with 6 members.
  - read_write on projects: Stores project information. Initially empty.
  - read_write on tasks: Stores task records. Initially empty.

  >>> Each child must declare a SUBSET of these global_vars in the 'global_vars' field. <<<
  >>> Children perform actual data operations; parent orchestrates by calling children. <<<

Maximum children allowed: 10
Maximum depth: 3
Return ONLY the JSON response.

children.name: CreateProject
children.purpose: Handle create_project command: create a new project after validation.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains name, description, owner_id.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store for owner existence check.

inputs.name: projects
inputs.description: Global project store to add new project.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success, message, and data (project_id).

in_scope: Creating project with auto-generated ID
Validating owner existence in members
Setting default status and creation time

children.behavior: Parses project_data, checks that owner_id exists in members store, generates a unique project_id (incrementing counter), sets status to 'planning', records current timestamp as creation_time, adds the project to projects store, and returns success with project_id.
children.signature: def CreateProject(project_data: dict, members: dict, projects: dict) -> dict

children.name: UpdateProject
children.purpose: Handle update_project command: update project fields after validation.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains project_id and optional fields to update.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store for owner existence check if owner_id is provided.

inputs.name: projects
inputs.description: Global project store to update project.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success, message, and updated project data.

in_scope: Updating description, owner, status, deadline of an existing project
Validating project existence and owner existence if changed

children.behavior: Parses project_data, validates project_id exists in projects store. If owner_id is provided, validates it exists in members. Updates only provided fields. Returns success message with updated project data.
children.signature: def UpdateProject(project_data: dict, members: dict, projects: dict) -> dict

children.name: DeleteProject
children.purpose: Handle delete_project command: delete project and all its tasks, releasing members.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains project_id.

inputs.name: projects
inputs.description: Global project store to remove project.

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store to delete associated tasks.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store to release members if tasks were assigned.

in_scope: Deleting project and all its tasks
Releasing members assigned to deleted tasks

children.behavior: Parses project_data, validates project exists. Retrieves all tasks with that project_id. For each task, if assigned, set member's availability to 'available' (or remove assignee). Delete all tasks from tasks store. Delete project from projects store. Returns success.
children.signature: def DeleteProject(project_data: dict, projects: dict, tasks: dict, members: dict) -> dict

children.name: CreateTask
children.purpose: Handle create_task command: create a new task under a project after validation.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains project_id, title, description, priority, estimated_hours.

inputs.name: projects
inputs.description: Global project store to verify project exists and status.

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store to add new task.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success, message, and data (task_id).

in_scope: Creating task with auto-generated ID
Validating project exists and status is planning or active
Setting default status to todo and no assignee

children.behavior: Parses project_data, validates project_id exists in projects and its status is 'planning' or 'active'. Generates unique task_id. Adds task with provided fields and defaults (status='todo', assignee_id=None). Returns success with task_id.
children.signature: def CreateTask(project_data: dict, projects: dict, tasks: dict) -> dict

children.name: AssignTask
children.purpose: Handle assign_task command: assign a task to a member after validation.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains task_id and assignee_id.

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store to update task assignee.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store to check member availability and update status.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success message and updated task/member data.

in_scope: Validating task exists and unassigned
Validating member exists and available
Optionally checking skills
Updating assignee and member status

children.behavior: Parses project_data, validates task_id exists in tasks and assignee_id is None. Validates member_id exists in members and availability is 'available'. Optionally checks skill match (if project_data has skills? Not specified, but we can ignore for simplicity or do basic check). Updates task's assignee_id, sets member's availability to 'busy'. Returns success.
children.signature: def AssignTask(project_data: dict, tasks: dict, members: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read member availability and update status

children.name: UpdateTaskStatus
children.purpose: Handle update_task_status command (non-done transitions): update task status following flow.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains task_id and status (one of in_progress, review, blocked).

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store to update task status.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success message and updated task.

in_scope: Validating task exists
Checking status transition rules (todo->in_progress, in_progress->review, any->blocked)
Updating status

children.behavior: Parses project_data, validates task_id exists. Checks current status. If new status is 'blocked', allow from any status. Otherwise, enforce flow: from 'todo' to 'in_progress', from 'in_progress' to 'review'. If invalid transition, return error. Updates task's status. Returns success.
children.signature: def UpdateTaskStatus(project_data: dict, tasks: dict) -> dict

children.name: CompleteTask
children.purpose: Handle complete_task command: mark task as done with actual hours and release member.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains task_id and actual_hours.

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store to update task status and hours.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store to release assignee.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success message and updated task.

in_scope: Validating task exists and status is 'review'
Updating status to 'done' and recording actual_hours
Releasing assignee (setting availability to 'available')

children.behavior: Parses project_data, validates task_id exists and current status is 'review'. Updates status to 'done', sets actual_hours. If task has assignee, sets that member's availability to 'available'. Returns success.
children.signature: def CompleteTask(project_data: dict, tasks: dict, members: dict) -> dict

children.name: DeleteTask
children.purpose: Handle delete_task command: delete a task and release member if assigned.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains task_id.

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store to delete task.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store to release assignee if any.

in_scope: Validating task exists
Releasing member if task was assigned
Deleting task

children.behavior: Parses project_data, validates task_id exists in tasks. If task has assignee, set that member's availability to 'available'. Delete task from tasks store. Returns success.
children.signature: def DeleteTask(project_data: dict, tasks: dict, members: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read and update member availability if needed

children.name: QueryChild
children.purpose: Handle list_project_tasks, get_member_tasks, get_project_progress commands: query data and return results.

inputs.name: command
inputs.description: One of 'list_project_tasks', 'get_member_tasks', 'get_project_progress'.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains relevant filters: project_id, member_id, filters etc.

inputs.name: projects
inputs.description: Global project store for progress queries.

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store for task queries.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store for member task queries.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success and data (list or progress info).

in_scope: Listing tasks with optional filters
Getting member tasks with hours calculation
Computing project progress (completion rate, hours, etc.)

children.behavior:
Based on command, executes query logic:
- list_project_tasks: filter by project_id, optional status/priority/assignee filters; return list of task details.
- get_member_tasks: find tasks with assignee_id = member_id; compute total hours (sum of estimated_hours for incomplete, actual_hours for completed).
- get_project_progress: count tasks by project_id; compute completion rate (done/total); status counts; sum estimated_hours vs actual_hours (for done tasks); estimated completion time (based on completion rate?).
Returns JSON with data.
children.signature: def QueryChild(command: str, project_data: dict, projects: dict, tasks: dict, members: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read project info for progress queries
Read tasks for listing and calculations
Read member info if needed (e.g., name in member tasks)

children.name: MemberChild
children.purpose: Handle add_member and update_member_availability commands: manage member data.

inputs.name: command
inputs.description: One of 'add_member', 'update_member_availability'.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Contains member_id and other fields.

inputs.name: members
inputs.description: Global member store to add/update members.

inputs.name: tasks
inputs.description: Global task store to check ongoing tasks for availability update.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON response with success message and data.

in_scope: Adding member with unique ID
Updating member availability with validation of no ongoing tasks

children.behavior: If command is 'add_member': validates member_id uniqueness, adds member with provided name, role, skills, availability (default 'available' if not provided). If command is 'update_member_availability': validates member exists, if setting availability to 'offline', checks that member has no tasks with status not in ['done', 'cancelled'] (i.e., ongoing task). Updates availability. Returns success.
children.signature: def MemberChild(command: str, project_data: dict, members: dict, tasks: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Check for ongoing tasks when updating availability

response.decomposition_rationale: The parent Project Task Manager is a command router that receives a JSON input with a 'command' field and corresponding data. To decompose, we identified 10 child functions that each handle a specific set of commands, grouping related operations to stay within the 10-child limit. Each child function encapsulates the validation and data manipulation for its commands, accessing the global data stores (members, projects, tasks) as needed. The parent calls exactly one child per input based on the command, and the child returns a JSON response. This decomposition ensures single responsibility, maintainability, and that no child calls another child, preserving the tree structure.
